message:
br-bd-elah2 evict FuseInodeLocks entries when last guard drops

FuseInodeGuard::drop now removes the table entry once the per-inode
Arc<FuseInodeLock> has only the table + this guard as references
(strong_count == 2). The eviction check is performed while holding
the table mutex, so no concurrent acquire() can observe an empty
entry between the strong_count read and the table mutation, and any
waiter that already cloned the Arc before entering the condvar wait
keeps strong_count >= 3 and blocks eviction.

Also threaded ino + Arc<FuseInodeLocks> into FuseInodeGuard so the
drop path can reach the owning table, and converted FuseInner's
inode_locks field from FuseInodeLocks to Arc<FuseInodeLocks> so
guards can retain a back-reference without widening the hot-path
dispatch cost.

Tests (ffs-fuse): 229 passed, 0 failed. Four new tests cover:
- drop eviction of the table entry
- sequential 100K inode churn stays bounded
- entry is retained while a waiter blocks on the held flag
- mutual-exclusion invariant preserved under 16-thread contention

Also closes bd-elah2 in .beads/issues.jsonl via direct JSONL edit
because br sync was wedged in a tight loop on the malformed
.beads/beads.db; per the ops_beads_db_corruption playbook JSONL is
the canonical recovery target and br will rebuild the DB later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Audit e2compr rewrite allocation/free logic and conformance fixture accounting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:00:56.472608017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:04:33.401953712Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:04:33.399841023Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed stale MVCC bitmap snapshots in compressed ext4 allocation/free paths and preserved orphan traversal via raw inode reads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2compr","ext4"]}

-{"id":"bd-elah2","title":"[MAINT] FuseInodeLocks.table grows unboundedly — every accessed inode allocates a FuseInodeLock that is never evicted","description":"# Maintainability / resource finding from modes-of-reasoning pass

## Finding

`FuseInodeLocks` at crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:703-737 stores a `Mutex<BTreeMap<InodeNumber, Arc<FuseInodeLock>>>`. Every FUSE mutation operation (create/mkdir/unlink/rmdir/rename/setattr) calls `acquire()`, which does `table.entry(ino).or_insert_with(...)` — inserting a new entry on first encounter and never evicting.

For a long-running FUSE mount that touches many inodes, the table grows monotonically. With ~80 bytes per entry (key + Arc + FuseInodeLock fields), a mount that accesses a million inodes parks ~80 MB of never-freed locks.

## Evidence

- Storage: crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:705 `table: Mutex<BTreeMap<InodeNumber, Arc<FuseInodeLock>>>`
- Insertion: line 727 `table.entry(ino).or_insert_with(|| Arc::new(FuseInodeLock::default()))`
- No `remove` call anywhere — confirmed via `rg 'table\.\(remove\|clear\|retain\)' crates/ffs-fuse/`
- No TTL, no LRU eviction, no drop-on-last-guard hook

## Why this wasn't caught earlier

Not a deadlock (inspected in /deadlock-finder pass, out of that skill's scope). Not a correctness bug. Pure resource accumulation that only matters for long-running mounts. Relevant as soon as FrankenFS targets real production workloads.

## Fix directions

1. **Drop-on-last-guard cleanup.** When `FuseInodeGuard::drop` runs, check if the `Arc<FuseInodeLock>` strong_count is 1 (we hold the last ref). If so, remove the entry from the table. Race-safe because `acquire()` holds the table mutex during insert, so we can hold it during the last-guard removal check.
2. **Epoch-based GC.** Periodically scan the table and evict entries whose `Arc` strong_count is 1 (no active guard) AND that haven't been acquired in N seconds.
3. **Cap-based eviction.** Soft cap the table size; evict idle entries LRU when the cap is exceeded.

Option 1 is cleanest and matches the natural lifecycle (entries exist only when a guard is live).

## Acceptance

- A stress test creating/unlinking 100K inodes shows FuseInodeLocks.table stays bounded (approximately proportional to concurrent guards, not to total inodes-ever-accessed).
- No regression in the sorted-ino total-order guarantee (the crate's only concurrency invariant). /deadlock-finder sweep remains CLEAN.
- Unit test: acquire+drop a single inode's guard, assert the table is empty afterward.

## Severity rationale

P3 (low) — not exploitable, not correctness, not currently a production concern (FrankenFS mounts are short-lived in V1.x). But it is a real resource leak that will matter when V2+ targets longer-running deployments. Worth tracking so it doesn't get lost.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:54.401068265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:54.401068265Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

But it is a real resource leak that will matter when V2+ targets longer-running deployments. Worth tracking so it doesn't get lost.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:54.401068265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:20:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:20:00Z","close_reason":"FuseInodeGuard::drop now evicts table entry when holding last external Arc (strong_count == 2); added 4 unit tests covering drop eviction, 100K sequential churn bound, waiter-preserves-entry invariant, and mutual-exclusion regression"}
 {"id":"bd-ente","title":"Unify duplicate golden verification scripts and docs onto canonical rch-backed gate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OrangeAnchor","created_at":"2026-04-05T23:48:00.857134035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T01:26:06.381481316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T01:26:06.381293580Z","close_reason":"Completed: deprecated verify-goldens shim now forwards to canonical rch-backed verify_golden gate; docs updated to point at canonical conformance flow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-eptw","title":"Repo hygiene: ignore local benchmark scratch artifacts","description":"Context: bv triage reports all existing beads closed (no actionable work). Current workspace repeatedly accumulates untracked local-only benchmark artifacts (loopback ext4 images, mount staging dirs, local sqlite backups) that create noise and accidental-commit risk for multi-agent work.\n\nScope:\n- Add targeted .gitignore rules for local benchmark scratch artifacts only.\n- Do not ignore tracked benchmark baselines or fixture assets.\n- Validate rules via git check-ignore on representative paths.\n\nAcceptance:\n- Existing untracked scratch paths are ignored by git.\n- Tracked baseline files remain tracked/unaffected.\n- Agent-mail update posted with bead id and completion summary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:53:17.689900464Z","created_by":"SilverLake","updated_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01.309714130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01.309693260Z","close_reason":"Completed: scratch artifact ignore rules added and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-coordination","benchmark","ci","hygiene"],"comments":[{"id":265,"issue_id":"bd-eptw","author":"SilverLake","text":"Implemented targeted .gitignore rules for benchmark scratch artifacts and local sqlite backups; validated with git check-ignore for mount_probe.ext4, mount_recovery_probe.ext4, mount_probe_mounts/, ffs_cli_inspect_probe.stderr, and storage.sqlite3.bak. Confirmed tracked baseline file baselines/hyperfine/20260218/ffs_cli_parity.json is not ignored.","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-etxp9","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for unlink rmdir and rename","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e covers unlink, rmdir, and rename success on writable mounts, but the mounted-path read-only refusal contract is not frozen even though ffs-fuse rejects these namespace mutations with EROFS before dispatch. Scope: add read-only btrfs probes for unlink, rmdir, and rename that assert exact EROFS and unchanged directory entries. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes read-only operator-visible refusal for unlink, rmdir, and rename.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:27:15.710372657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:57:48.915853489Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:57:48.915853489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
